#2919 — Session-Scoped Hook State (cross-session bleed fix)

Playground: before/after model of the five hooks whose shared project state leaked between concurrent Claude Code sessions.

The disease

Session A ──┐ ┌── Session A reads sees B + C too Session B ──┼──▶ .claude/state/* ──▶┼── Session B reads sees A + C too Session C ──┘ (no session key) └── Session C reads sees A + B too

Live symptom that motivated the fix: a session that never touched SKILL.md received repeated "SKILL.md: 3 edits in the last 10 events" thrash warnings — the edits belonged to the previous day's session.

The cure — finish a pattern the codebase already had

WRITE stamp session_id on every record (goal-history.jsonl already did this) READ filter to the current session (expect-skip.<sid> already did this)

Per-hook fix map

State fileSeverityBroken behaviorFixLegacy records
edit-history.jsonlhighthrash-detector counted every session's editsrecords gain sid; window = last 10 events of this sessionnever match; age out via 50-cap
goal-budget-tripped.jsonhighany session's brake hard-blocked /goal everywhereread-side: block only when brake.session_id matchesstop blocking strangers immediately
worktree-advisory-*.mdmediumfirst session to prompt stole + deleted any advisoryfilename gains .<sid>.md; consumer takes own filesun-keyed files consumable by anyone (no orphans)
last-test-run.jsonmediumanother session's npm test satisfied "this session's" gaterecord gains session_id; mismatch = no run this sessiontreated as not-this-session (honest)
expect-auto-fires.jsonmediumone session's cooldown swallowed another's nudgemap key sid:route; entries >1h prunednever match; pruned within the hour

Verification

PASS vitest 290 files / 7,179 tests (13 new #2919 regression cases) PASS typecheck tsc --noEmit clean PASS biome 606 files clean

New regression coverage per hook: cross-session isolation (other session's records ignored) + legacy-record behavior (pre-upgrade files degrade safely).

OrchestKit · fixes #2919 · five hooks, one principle: a session only trusts its own state.
